spec_match.rationale: The harness now tests the current readonly numeric navigator.cpuPerformance shape instead of the obsolete navigator.cpu.performance() function form.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP ran all 12 tests, expanded a result row, and observed live summary counts and log output.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature index and page both describe API availability, value shape, repeated reads, fallbacks, and companion signals.

edge_cases.rationale: The harness covers unsupported API fallback, secure context, repeated reads, reduced motion, tier-gated routing, Compute Pressure pairing, and a visible entropy budget for CPU tier plus companion hints.

references.rationale: The concept page now embeds both the ChromeStatus feature link and WICG CPU Performance draft.

design_system.rationale: A follow-up scan found no raw hex colors in the CPU concept HTML, and this page uses shared variables for status colors.

summary: The safety harness now matches navigator.cpuPerformance, adds a fingerprinting entropy panel, and carries the canonical references. In Headless Chrome 151, DevTools MCP reported 7 pass, 5 warn for unsupported origin-trial CPU API paths, 0 fail, and no console errors.
